generated_from: scripts/scrape-cannes-categories.json — Phase 1 baseline; refine after initial-scrape data is in

cannes.palme_dor.notes: Palme d'Or — single winner per ceremony in modern era. Tolerance 1 allows for the rare hypothetical tie.

cannes.grand_prix.notes: Grand Prix (runner-up to Palme d'Or). Single winner expected; tolerance 1 for ties.

cannes.jury_prize.notes: Jury Prize. Frequently tied at Cannes — observed 2-way ties (cer 2000) and 3-way ties (cer 2019, 2025). Tolerance 3 covers the historical 1-3 winner range.

cannes.best_director.notes: Best Director. Person-typed; co-direction credits split into separate rows per split_co_recipients=true.

cannes.best_actor.notes: Best Actor. Tied at Cannes occasionally (e.g. cer 2010: Bardem + Germano); also team awards (cer 2006 Days of Glory — 5 actors split into 5 rows). Tolerance 5 covers historical 1-5 winner range observed in initial scrape.

cannes.best_actress.notes: Best Actress. Occasionally tied or awarded as a team (cer 2024 — 4 actresses for one film, cer 2006 Volver — 6 actresses for one film, both split into separate rows). Tolerance 6 covers historical 1-6 winner range observed in initial scrape.

cannes.best_screenplay.notes: Best Screenplay. Generally single winner; co-writers kept on one row per split_co_recipients=false.

cannes.camera_dor.notes: Caméra d'Or (best first feature). Cer 2000 was a tie; tolerance 1 covers.

cannes.short_film_palme_dor.notes: Short Film Palme d'Or. Single winner expected; tolerance 1 for occasional ties.
